    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (unsupported empirical claim).",

rationale.R1.5: Proposes a coherent end-to-end section plan with per-section rationale.
rationale.R1.6: Reads as a careful copy-edit pass with consistent register, no lost meaning.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., 'broadly competitive', 'in principle').
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (unsupported empirical claim).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (missing in this artifact).
